Confirmed in scenario_kapoor_family.txt Section 2: 'Full Name: Meera Kapoor', SIN 923-456-789, DOB July 8, 1981
Confirmed in scenario_kapoor_family.txt Section 3. Rohan has T2201 DTC certificate approved. Anika attends daycare ($12,800/year, capped at $8,000).
Confirmed in scenario: 'Province of Residence: British Columbia (as of December 31, 2024)' for both Arjun and Meera. Address: 2847 West 15th Avenue, Vancouver, BC V6K 2Z1.
Confirmed in scenario Section 4A: Arjun 'rented a shared office space in Calgary, AB from January to December 2024 (secondary establishment)'. Alberta clients revenue: $68,250.
Arithmetic verified: 390000-50000-12000 = 328000. QSBC shares of Kapoor Consulting Inc. (CCPC), sold May 28, 2024. Full LCGE claim eliminates tax on this gain.
CRA Line 25400 page confirms 2024 Period 1 deduction limit as 50% of $1,016,813 = $508,418. Original scenario had $1,016,836 (off by $23), corrected to $1,016,813.
Arithmetic verified: 126750/195000 = 0.65 (65.0%), 68250/195000 = 0.35 (35.0%). 126750+68250 = 195000. Original scenario had 64.5%/35.5% (inconsistent with dollar amounts), corrected to 65.0%/35.0%.

pi_items.metadata_value: ITA s.1100(11) prohibits CCA from creating/increasing net rental loss
pi_items.verified_value: Correct. Combined rental is -$2,640 loss (Property 1: +$6,260, Property 2: -$8,900). CCA must NOT be claimed.
pi_items.source: CRA IT-195R4 and T4036 Guide confirm Reg. 1100(11) restriction. Arithmetic: 26400-20140=6260; 8400-17300=-8900; 6260+(-8900)=-2640. All expense totals verified by summing individual items.

CRA 2024 Federal Worksheet 5000-D1 confirms DTC base $9,872 and under-18 supplement $5,758. Original scenario had $14,928 (using 2023 figures: $9,428+$5,500), corrected to $15,630. Supplement reduction threshold confirmed as $3,373 (not $3,427).

pi_items.metadata_value: donations pooled on Arjun, medical on Meera, childcare on Meera, DTC to Arjun
pi_items.verified_value: All four optimization decisions are correct per CRA rules
pi_items.source: Donations: pooling on higher-income spouse maximizes credit above $200 threshold (29%+). Medical: 3% of Meera's ~$40,210 net = ~$1,206 threshold vs Arjun's $2,759 cap. Childcare: ITA s.63 requires lower-income spouse. DTC: non-refundable credit best used by higher-tax spouse. All verified against CRA Income Tax Folios.

pi_items.metadata_value: Box 21 CG $4,600; Box 23 eligible dividends $3,200; Box 25 foreign income $2,845 CAD; Box 34 foreign tax $427 CAD
pi_items.verified_value: All amounts verified. Foreign conversion: $2,100 USD × 1.3550 = $2,845.50 (rounded to $2,845). $315 USD × 1.3550 = $426.83 (rounded to $427). Eligible dividend gross-up at 38% = $4,416.
pi_items.source: Arithmetic verified. CRA eligible dividend gross-up rate of 38% confirmed via Income Tax Folio S3-F2-C2.

CRA T778 form and Line 21400 guidance confirm $8,000 annual limit per eligible child under 7 for 2024. Actual paid is $12,800, capped at $8,000.

privileged_info_summary: This is a coupled spousal tax return for Arjun and Meera Kapoor (British Columbia, 2024). ARJUN: Self-employed consultant (Kapoor Strategic Consulting) with T2125 gross revenue $195,000 (BC $126,750 = 65%, AB $68,250 = 35%), expenses $39,600, net $155,400. Requires Form T2203 for BC/AB interprovincial allocation. Sold QSBC shares of Kapoor Consulting Inc. on May 28, 2024: proceeds $390,000, ACB $50,000, selling expenses $12,000, capital gain $328,000. Must claim full LCGE via Form T657 (2024 limit $1,016,813) at Line 25400 to eliminate tax on the gain. T3 trust income: capital gains $4,600, eligible dividends $3,200 (grossed-up $4,416 at 38%), foreign income $2,845 CAD ($2,100 USD at 1.3550), foreign tax paid $427 CAD — requires Form T2209. RRSP $22,000. Charitable donations $5,400 (pooled with Meera's $2,800 = $8,200 total on Arjun's return). MEERA: T4 employment income $48,600 from Vancouver Coastal Health (CPP $2,593.50, EI $1,002.45, RPP $4,860, tax deducted $7,290, union dues $890). Two rental properties on T776: Property 1 (Coquitlam condo) gross $26,400, expenses $20,140, net +$6,260. Property 2 (Kelowna cabin) gross $8,400, expenses $17,300, net -$8,900. Combined rental loss -$2,640. CRITICAL: CCA must NOT be claimed (ITA Reg. 1100(11) prohibits CCA from creating/increasing rental loss). Medical expenses $6,830 total claimed on Meera's return (lower 3% threshold: ~$1,206 vs Arjun's $2,759). Childcare $8,000 for Anika (capped from $12,800 actual; ITA s.63 lower-income spouse rule). FAMILY: DTC for Rohan ($15,630 = base $9,872 + under-18 supplement $5,758) transferred to Arjun. Output file: kapoor_family.24t in C:\Users\Docker\Documents\StudioTax\.
